Add fuzzy case/accent-insensitive name matching for OCR member identification

Replace all 6 exact-substring name comparisons with _names_match() which:
- Normalizes to lowercase and strips accents (é→e, ô→o, etc.)
- Checks substring containment first
- Falls back to SequenceMatcher fuzzy ratio (≥80% threshold)

This correctly matches 'MARYLINE LAMARCHE' to 'Marylin Lamarche' (97% similar)
and 'HÉLOÏSE CÔTÉ' to 'Heloise Cote'.

Applies to both scan session review and single-receipt review paths.

commit.message:
Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution (#203)

* Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution

B4c uses G4OCCTSolid for the absorber and gap shapes (STEP-imported), making
it the ideal vehicle for profiling realistic G4OCCTSolid call patterns across
all five navigation methods: Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), DistanceToOut(p,v),
DistanceToIn(p) [safety], DistanceToOut(p) [safety].

Changes:

src/examples/B4c/CMakeLists.txt:
- Add option(B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS ON): allows CI to pass OFF to prevent placement
  overlap checking from inflating Inside() call counts during construction.
- target_compile_definitions: B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=<1|0> passed to the compiler
  so DetectorConstruction.hh can use it as a default member initializer.
- if(VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR): add HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H + include path for
  exampleB4c, enabling CALLGRIND macros in RunAction.

src/examples/B4c/include/DetectorConstruction.hh:
- fCheckOverlaps default initializer changed from true to B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS
  so the CI profiling job disables it at compile time without touching source.

src/examples/B4c/src/RunAction.cc:
- Add CALLGRIND guard block (HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H) at top.
- BeginOfRunAction: CALLGRIND_START_INSTRUMENTATION + CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT
  -> geometry construction and physics init excluded from profile.
- EndOfRunAction:   CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT + CALLGRIND_STOP_INSTRUMENTATION.

src/examples/B4c/run_profile.mac (new):
- 100 events, e- 300 MeV, /run/verbose 0.  Enough statistics for stable
  per-method call ratios; with --instr-atstart=no, runtime is manageable.

.github/workflows/ci.yml:
- New callgrind-b4c job: builds exampleB4c with B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF and
  VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR, runs run_profile.mac under callgrind with gated
  instrumentation, annotates with callgrind_annotate, uploads artifact
  callgrind-reports-b4c (annotated txt + raw .out, 7-day retention).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Extend CI callgrind profiling to all geometry families and assembly (#198)

* Extend CI callgrind profiling to all geometry families and assembly

Geometry (bench_callgrind matrix):
- Add 6 new matrix entries — one fixture per remaining family:
  - g4unionsolid-box-overlap-x10-v1    (boolean-compound)
  - g4tessellatedsolid-tessellated-tet-right-20x30x40-v1 (tessellated)
  - g4twistedbox-box-dx10-dy8-z20-phi30-v1 (twisted-swept)
  - g4ellipsoid-ellipsoid-15x10x20-v1  (profile-faceted)
  - g4displacedsolid-translated-box-offset-v1 (wrapper-decorator)
  - nist-ctc-01-v1                     (nist-ctc; STEP fetch step
    added conditionally via if: contains(matrix.fixture.id,'nist-ctc'))
- Each matrix entry produces a separate artifact
  callgrind-reports-<name> for independent inspection.

Assembly (new callgrind-assembly job):
- Uses bench_assembly_navigator in single-fixture mode on
  string-array-v1 (repeated-component tracker-like geometry).
- Single-fixture mode loads only one fixture so GDML parse / STEP
  import / geometry construction are confined to that fixture.
- CALLGRIND macros added to bench_assembly_navigator.cc around
  benchmark::RunSpecifiedBenchmarks() so those preloading steps
  are excluded from the profile (same pattern as bench_callgrind).
- --benchmark_iterations=1 limits Google Benchmark to one iteration
  per variant to keep the callgrind run within CI time budget.
- Uploads artifact callgrind-reports-assembly (raw + annotated).

CMake:
- bench_assembly_navigator now also receives HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H
  and the VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR include path when the headers are found,
  inside the existing if(VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR) block.

commit.message:
Add GPT Vision OCR to receipt upload on existing bons

When uploading a receipt to an existing bon de commande, the file now
goes through the same GPT Vision analysis pipeline used in the scan
session flow. After OCR, the user is redirected to a review page to
verify and confirm the extracted data.

- ReceiptUploadToExistingView now calls ReceiptOcrService.process_receipts_batch()
- New ReceiptReviewSingleView for reviewing OCR data on a single receipt
- New receipt_review_single.html template with side-by-side receipt/form view
- New URL: /bons/<bon_pk>/receipts/<receipt_pk>/review/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Security hardening: auth, headers, validation, isolation

CRITICAL fixes:
- Add ViewerRequiredMixin to all financial read views (budget, bons, members)
  No more anonymous access to any data
- Add house filtering (_filter_by_house) to list/detail querysets
  Prevents IDOR cross-house data leakage
- Prevent double-validation in BonValidateView (status check before
  creating Expense, blocks duplicate entries)

HIGH fixes:
- Security headers: X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff,
  SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY, CSRF/SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE (via DJANGO_REQUIRE_HTTPS)
- Server-side file upload validation: type (PDF/JPEG/PNG only) + size (10MB max)
- Random passwords for auto-created house accounts (secrets.token_urlsafe)
- Path traversal protection in receipt_upload_to filename sanitization
- MinValueValidator(0) on Expense.amount to prevent negative amounts
- Password minimum length increased from 8 to 12 characters
- Django DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE and FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE limits

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix balance recalc: add cache-busting + recalc on filter

Browser was caching old tables.js without recalcRunningBalances.
Added ?v=2 query parameter to static file URLs.

commit.message:
Replace faceted STEP approximations with exact analytic surfaces for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid (#189)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace faceted lofts with exact analytic NURBS for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid

Replace the 24-segment faceted circular loft STEP files for G4Hype and
G4Paraboloid with exact analytic NURBS solids built from true conic
sections, and remove the corresponding xfail.

The old approach used polyline sections through profile samples fed into
thrusections. The resulting polyhedral surfaces were fundamentally
incompatible with the sub-nanometre ray-comparison tolerance.

New approach (DRAWEXE):
- G4Paraboloid: parabola() with focal F=k/4=1.8 (from r²=7.2·(z+25)),
  trim to t∈[6,18], revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.
- G4Hype: hyperbola() with a=8, b=8/tan(20°), trim to u∈[±asinh(25/b)],
  revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.

nurbsconvert converts the SURFACE_OF_REVOLUTION (PARABOLA/HYPERBOLA)
to degree-(2,2) RATIONAL_B_SPLINE_SURFACE — the same format used by
the G4Ellipsoid fixture — which the OCCT STEPControl_Reader can read.

The resulting surfaces are geometrically exact: ray comparisons pass at
the Geant4 surface tolerance (≈0.5×10⁻⁹ mm). Volume expectations are
updated to the analytic values; tolerance is 1.0 mm³ to accommodate
BRepGProp Gauss-quadrature integration variance over NURBS.

commit.message:
Remove 117 redundant tests identified by coverage fingerprinting

Per-test instrumented coverage analysis revealed that 715 of 855 tests
cover zero unique Rust source lines. Within that set, 119 tests share
identical coverage fingerprints with another test in the same class —
meaning they exercise the exact same code paths with trivially different
inputs.

Two tests (get_missing_with_default for Document and DictProxy) were
restored after discovering they cover a distinct closure/region within
a shared line — a case where line-level coverage misses sub-line
branching.

Methodology:
- Built instrumented .so with RUSTFLAGS="-Cinstrument-coverage"
- Ran each of 855 tests individually, collecting per-test (file, line) sets
- Grouped tests by identical coverage fingerprint
- Within each same-class group, kept one representative and removed the rest
- Verified: 738 tests pass, line coverage 98.38% (unchanged)

commit.message:
fix: use path-agnostic gcovr filters to fix zero coverage report (#190)

The previous filter anchored on the absolute path returned by
realpath(1) inside the Singularity container:

  --filter "${ROOT}/src/G4OCCT.*"
  --filter "${ROOT}/include/G4OCCT/.*"

Inside the eic-shell Apptainer container the workspace is accessible
via a bind-mount whose path may not round-trip through realpath(1)
identically to the path that cmake (which calls realpath(3) internally
via cmSystemTools::CollapseFullPath) records in .gcno files.  When the
two diverge, gcovr emits "All coverage data is filtered out" and the
report shows 0 / 0 for every metric.

Replace the absolute-path filters with path-agnostic patterns that
start with / (so gcovr treats them as absolute-path regexes) but use
.* to absorb whatever prefix the build system used:

  --filter '/.*/src/G4OCCT[^/]*'
  --filter '/.*/include/G4OCCT/.*'

These match project library source and header files regardless of the
concrete absolute path in use inside the container.  The --root option
(which only controls how paths are displayed in the HTML report, not
which files are included) continues to use realpath(1).

commit.message:
Per-fixture process isolation for assembly GDML/STEP benchmarks (#187)

* Initial plan

* Fix barrel-tracker-v1 geometry.gdml to use unique volume and material names

Both barrel-tracker-v1 and triple-box-v1 used identical names for their
world logical volume ("WorldLV"), world solid ("WorldBox"), and air material
("G4_AIR"). When G4GDMLParser::GetWorldVolume() searches the global Geant4
stores by name, it could return triple-box's world for barrel-tracker's
benchmark, causing it to compare cylinders (STEP) against boxes (GDML).

Rename to fixture-unique names:
  - "WorldBox"  → "BarrelTrackerWorldBox"
  - "WorldLV"   → "BarrelTrackerWorldLV"
  - "G4_AIR"    → "BT_AIR"  (also avoids NIST material-name conflicts)
  - element "N" → "BT_N"
  - element "O" → "BT_O"
  - setup world ref updated to "BarrelTrackerWorldLV"
  - provenance.yaml updated to mention "BT_AIR"

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/15e35f41-3799-4a5e-b3ff-3196b76e3127

* Add per-fixture process isolation to assembly benchmark

Run bench_assembly_navigator once per fixture so each gets a clean Geant4
global state (material table, solid store, logical-volume store), preventing
cross-fixture name conflicts without requiring workarounds in fixture files.

bench_assembly_navigator.cc:
- Add single-fixture mode: when fixture_root itself contains geometry.gdml
  and shape.step, treat it as the fixture dir (not a parent to scan). CI
  invokes the binary once per fixture dir in this mode.
- Extract RegisterFixture lambda with explicit captures for clarity.
- Update docstring to document both single-fixture and multi-fixture modes.

ci.yml:
- Replace single all-fixtures invocation with a for-loop over fixture dirs.
- Each invocation writes bench_assembly_raw_<fixture_id>.json.
- Per-fixture failures warn but continue (benchmarks are not gatekeepers).

generate_assembly_report.py:
- Accept a glob pattern as first arg (bench_assembly_raw_*.json).
- Expand the glob and merge benchmark entries from all matching JSON files.
- Single-file usage (literal path) remains backward compatible.

barrel-tracker-v1/geometry.gdml:
- Update comment on unique element/material names to clarify they are kept
  for backward compatibility in multi-fixture/local development mode.

commit.message:
Fix accept/decline friend request: use setQueryData instead of invalidateQueries

The IDB offline-first cache (gcTime: 7 days) causes a race where
invalidateQueries triggers a refetch but the persisted stale data
renders before the fresh result arrives, making the UI appear frozen.

Fix: replace invalidateQueries with setQueryData for instant updates:
- acceptMutation: pass full FriendRequest to mutation; on success,
  filter it out of ['friend-requests'] and append to ['friends']
- declineMutation: on success, filter declined request from
  ['friend-requests']

Same pattern as CharacterSelectPage create/delete fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix friend requests: auto-poll every 30s, add notification icons

- FriendsPage: add refetchInterval: 30_000 to friend-requests query
  so the recipient's Requests tab automatically refreshes and shows
  incoming requests without requiring a manual page reload.
- NotificationBell: add FriendRequest (👥) and FriendAccepted (✅)
  to TYPE_ICON map so friend notifications display meaningful icons
  instead of the generic bell fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix sw.js cache headers; sync Caddyfile to VPS state (Let's Encrypt default)

- Program.cs: serve sw.js with Cache-Control: no-cache so mobile
  browsers always check for a new service worker, preventing stale
  SW from blocking PWA updates or installs.
- Caddyfile: default is now VPS/Let's Encrypt mode (no local_certs
  or tls internal). Dev/LAN mode lines are commented out with
  instructions. Matches what has been running on the VPS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PWA install: conditional preventDefault, maskable icons, rename branding

- InstallPromptBanner: only call e.preventDefault() when the banner
  will actually be shown. If the user previously dismissed the banner
  (localStorage flag set), the event is not consumed so the browser's
  native install UI (Edge address bar button, etc.) still works.
- Fix banner text: 'TabletopSpells' -> 'Chronicle'
- vite.config: add purpose: 'any maskable' to both icon entries so
  Android adaptive icon masking works correctly on all launchers.

Security: gitignore docker-compose.override.yml to prevent postgres exposure on VPS

docker-compose.override.yml was committed to git, meaning the VPS would
pull it and expose postgres port 5432 publicly. Docker bypasses UFW on
Linux, so this was a real security vulnerability.

Cast jsonb columns to ::text before comparing to empty string literals.

syntax for type json' error on fresh database migrations.

Enforce non-null `fShape` invariant in `G4OCCTSolid` (#185)

* Initial plan

* Enforce non-null fShape invariant in G4OCCTSolid

The constructor and SetOCCTShape() now throw std::invalid_argument when
given a null TopoDS_Shape, establishing a class invariant that fShape is
always non-null. All defensive fShape.IsNull() guards in method bodies
are removed as dead code. Documentation updated throughout to reflect the

that `specify init` works without any network access by default.

Changes:

  version to 0.2.1

  port of generate_commands() shell function), and scaffold_from_core_pack();

  flag to opt back in to the GitHub download path

Closes #1711
Addresses #1752

- Embed release scripts (bash + PowerShell) in wheel via pyproject.toml
- Replace Python _generate_agent_commands() with subprocess invocation of
  the canonical create-release-packages.sh, guaranteeing byte-for-byte
  parity between 'specify init --offline' and GitHub release ZIPs

demos where ghost-text completions are distracting.

inline history suggestions disabled by default

```bash
flyline --disable-inline-history
```

feat: add G4OCCTMaterialMapReader for XML-driven material bridging

Implements §2.1 of docs/material_bridging.md: an explicit user-provided
material map that associates STEP material names with Geant4 G4Material*
objects via a lightweight XML file.

## Design

G4OCCTMaterialMapReader subclasses G4GDMLReadStructure, gaining zero-
duplication access to all G4GDMLReadMaterials protected parsing methods
(MaterialRead, ElementRead, IsotopeRead, FractionRead, DRead, …).
Only ~125 lines of new dispatch code are introduced.

## XML format

Two entry types are supported:

  <material stepName="AISI 316L" geant4Name="G4_STAINLESS-STEEL"/>

  <element name="Si" Z="14"><atom value="28.085"/></element>
  <material stepName="FR4" name="FR4" state="solid">
    <D value="1.86" unit="g/cm3"/>
    <fraction n="0.18" ref="Si"/>
    ...
  </material>

## Usage

  G4OCCTMaterialMapReader reader;
  G4OCCTMaterialMap map = reader.ReadFile("materials.xml");
  auto* assembly = G4OCCTAssemblyVolume::FromSTEP("detector.step", map);

## Files

- include/G4OCCT/G4OCCTMaterialMapReader.hh — class declaration with Doxygen
- src/G4OCCTMaterialMapReader.cc            — two-pass Xerces DOM implementation
- src/tests/test_material_map_reader.cc     — GoogleTest unit tests
- src/tests/CMakeLists.txt                  — test registration
- src/examples/B4c/materials.xml            — illustrative example
- docs/material_bridging.md                 — §2.1 marked implemented
